attacking_style: Prefers attacking on final climbs from breakaways rather than GC group, often going long-range (4km+ out) but lacks sustained acceleration to drop in-form climbers. Effective on ultra-steep gradients (20%+) but struggles to sustain repeated accelerations.
team_tactics: AG2R/DSM frequently places him in early breakaways with multiple teammates for numbers advantage. Team sets brutal tempo on penultimate climbs to fracture field before his attack. Selective energy management—contests KOM points aggressively even when compromising stage positioning.
terrain_preference: Excels on ultra-steep short pitches (20%+ gradients) and altitude finishes where single accelerations suffice. Ardennes-style finishes produce consistent podiums. Exposed badly on flat time trials (regularly loses 1:30-2:00) and cobbled sectors despite damage control skills.
vulnerability: Time trials are catastrophic weakness (lost 2min+ Dauphine 2017, 20s+ on 6.6km prologue). Vulnerable to illness/heat in Grand Tours (abandoned Giro 2022 4th GC, illness concerns Vuelta 2023). Poor positioning causes crashes on flat stages. Cannot sustain repeated accelerations against in-form climbers.
key_patterns: Attacks from breakaways rather than GC group, winning stages via early infiltration (Vuelta 2021x2)
Aggressive KOM jersey hunting even when compromising GC/stage tactics, contests intermediate sprints selectively (6+ instances)
Loses 1:30-2:00 consistently on flat time trials, exposing exploitable GC weakness (Dauphine 2017/2018, Oman 2017)
Third-week Grand Tour strength—form peaks late (Giro 2021 resurgence, Vuelta stage win 2021)
Poor tactical awareness in finales—attacks without monitoring rivals, gifting bonus seconds (Suisse 2023, Fleche 2023)
Team places multiple riders in early breaks for numbers/energy conservation (DSM Vuelta 2021, AG2R Dauphine 2018)
form_trajectory: neutral: 2023 showed attacking instincts returning (Suisse aggressive form) but tactical errors persist and illness concerns continue. Podium at Vuelta stage (3rd Javalambre) suggests climbing maintained but lacks winning punch, relegated to domestique-quality support roles by September.
